questions.question: What is the 'Demo Trap' described in the lesson?

options.text: The demo software crashing during a presentation
Assuming that showing the product features will automatically make the prospect understand the value
Showing the pricing page too early
Forgetting to record the meeting

questions.explanation: Technical founders often over-rely on the product to do the selling. They skip understanding the customer's problem and jump straight to 'Look at my code', which usually leads to ghosting.

questions.question: Why is 'Discovery' described as a filter?

options.text: It filters out spam emails
It protects your time by identifying who is actually qualified to buy
It filters the audio on Zoom calls
It filters your feature list

questions.explanation: Discovery allows you to disqualify bad prospects early. Instead of doing 47 demos for people with no budget, you only demo for the 5 who can buy.

questions.question: Which Discovery failure mode involves asking rapid-fire questions without building rapport?

options.text: The Skipper
The Interrogator
The Surface-Skimmer
The Closer

questions.explanation: The Interrogator gets the data but loses the relationship. The prospect feels processed and becomes defensive.

questions.question: In the '$5k vs $50k' case study, what changed the outcome?

options.text: The founder lowered the price
The founder added a new feature
The founder uncovered the financial cost of the problem (lost ad revenue)
The founder used a better slide deck

questions.explanation: The product and price remained the same. The difference was that Discovery revealed the *Cost of Inaction* ($200k loss), making the $50k price tag seem cheap by comparison.

questions.question: What is 'Information Asymmetry' in sales?

options.text: When the salesperson talks more than the prospect
When the prospect knows their budget/pain/timeline, but you don't
When the marketing materials are confusing
When the contract is one-sided

questions.explanation: Buyers start with all the leverage because they hold the information. Discovery is the process of extracting that information to level the playing field.
